channel.title: AI Explained
channel.description: Daily bite-sized episodes explaining AI concepts — from quantization to transformers. Narrated by Boti.
channel.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Benchmarks — How We Measure AI Intelligence
item.description: What do MMLU, GPQA, and SWE-bench actually test? We break down how AI benchmarks work, why they matter, and why you shouldn't trust any single score.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Distillation — Shrinking Smart Models into Small Ones
item.description: How knowledge distillation transfers the intelligence of massive AI models into smaller, faster ones you can run on a phone. We cover soft targets, temperature scaling, teacher-student training, and real-world examples like DistilBERT.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Agents and Tool Use — AI That Can Take Actions
item.description: Language models can generate impressive text, but agents take it further — they can actually do things. This episode explains how tool use and function calling turn language models into systems that perceive, act, and adapt in the real world.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Chain of Thought — Teaching AI to Reason Step by Step
item.description: How a simple prompting trick — asking AI to show its work — unlocked reasoning capabilities in large language models, from Jason Wei's 2022 paper to modern reasoning models like o1.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Hallucinations — Why AI Makes Things Up
item.description: Why do AI models confidently state things that are completely false? We explore the mechanics behind AI hallucinations — from statistical pattern matching to training data gaps — and what techniques like RAG, RLHF, and chain-of-thought are doing to fight back.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: GPUs vs CPUs — Why AI Needs Special Hardware
item.description: Why does AI training require GPUs instead of regular CPUs? We explore parallel computing, Tensor Cores, memory bandwidth, and how the gaming industry accidentally built the perfect AI chip.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Backpropagation — How AI Learns from Mistakes
item.description: The algorithm that makes neural network training possible. We break down how backpropagation uses the chain rule to propagate errors backwards through a network, computing gradients efficiently so every weight gets nudged in the right direction.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Neural Network Basics — Layers, Weights, and Activations
item.description: What actually is a neural network? We break it down into its three core building blocks — layers that give structure, weights that store learned knowledge, and activation functions that make deep learning possible.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Diffusion Models — How AI Generates Images
item.description: How do tools like Stable Diffusion and DALL-E create images from text? We break down the elegant idea behind diffusion models: learn to add noise, then run the process in reverse. From denoising basics to latent diffusion and text conditioning, this episode explains the technology powering AI image generation.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Mixture of Experts — Why Some Models Are Huge but Fast
item.description: How Mixture of Experts lets AI models have billions of parameters but only use a fraction at a time — giving you the intelligence of a huge model with the speed of a small one.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: RLHF — How AI Learns Human Preferences
item.description: How do you teach AI what 'good' means when you can't write it as a formula? RLHF lets humans show their preferences through comparisons, training a reward model that guides language models toward more helpful, safer, and more natural responses. We break down the full pipeline — from human rankings to reward models to reinforcement learning — and explore why this technique powers ChatGPT, Claude, and the modern AI assistants you use every day.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Prompt Engineering — Talking to AI Effectively
item.description: The art and science of crafting effective prompts for AI models. We cover role assignment, few-shot and zero-shot prompting, chain-of-thought reasoning, context engineering, and practical tips for getting better results from any language model.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Context Windows — How Much AI Can Remember at Once
item.description: What is a context window, and why does it matter? We explore the fixed-size memory that limits how much text an AI model can consider at once — from tiny 1K-token windows to today's two-million-token giants — and why bigger isn't always better.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: LoRA and Adapters — Cheap Fine-Tuning for Everyone
item.description: How LoRA lets you customize massive AI models by training just a tiny fraction of their parameters. The clever math behind low-rank adaptation, and why it democratized fine-tuning.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Temperature and Sampling — Why AI Responses Vary
item.description: Ever wonder why AI gives different answers to the same question? This episode explores temperature, top-k, top-p sampling, and the math behind how language models choose their words — from greedy decoding to creative chaos.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Embeddings — How AI Understands Meaning as Numbers
item.description: What are embeddings and why do they matter? We explore how AI converts words, sentences, and documents into lists of numbers that capture meaning — enabling semantic search, recommendations, and RAG. From Word2Vec to modern transformers, discover the bridge between human meaning and machine math.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) — Giving AI a Reference Library
item.description: How RAG lets AI models look things up instead of guessing. We cover vector databases, semantic search, the three-step pipeline of indexing, retrieval, and generation — and why this technique is behind most reliable AI assistants today.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Fine-Tuning vs Pre-Training — Teaching Old Models New Tricks
item.description: What's the difference between pre-training and fine-tuning a language model? We break down the two-stage process behind modern AI: the expensive foundation-building phase and the affordable customization step that makes models actually useful.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Attention Mechanism — How Models Focus on What Matters
item.description: From the cocktail party effect to the Transformer revolution: how attention lets AI models decide what's important in a sea of information. We break down queries, keys, values, multi-head attention, and why this single idea powers every major language model today.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Transformers — The architecture behind modern AI
item.description: How does the architecture powering GPT, Claude, and every major AI model actually work? We break down the transformer — from the limitations of older recurrent networks, to the self-attention mechanism that lets models process entire sentences at once. Queries, keys, values, multi-head attention, and why this 2017 paper changed everything.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Tokenization — How AI reads text
item.description: How do language models convert human text into numbers they can process? We explore tokenization — from simple character mapping to sophisticated subword algorithms like BPE. Why the word 'tokenization' might be split into multiple pieces, and why this invisible step shapes everything about AI text processing.
item.itunes:author: Boti

item.title: Quantization — How AI Models Shrink to Fit Your Laptop
item.description: What is quantization? How does it let you run massive AI models on a laptop with 8GB of RAM? We break down the math, the formats (GGUF, GPTQ, AWQ), and why neural networks are surprisingly tolerant of rounding.
item.itunes:author: Boti
